findings.evidence: `MarkingSwap.lean:6` imports `TauCeti.KnotTheory.Grid.GradingInteger`; `MarkingSwap.lean:57`, `:63`, and `:70` prove only facts about `maslovOℤ`, `maslovXℤ`, and `alexanderTwoℤ`; `GradingInteger.lean:137`, `:143`, and `:150` already house the analogous integer transpose facts.
findings.fix: Move these three theorems into `TauCeti/KnotTheory/Grid/GradingInteger.lean` near the existing integer transpose facts, and delete the now-empty `MarkingSwap.lean` unless it gains genuinely later material.
findings.issue: `maslovOℤ_swapMarkings`, `maslovXℤ_swapMarkings`, and `alexanderTwoℤ_swapMarkings` are integer-grading facts but live in a separate `MarkingSwap.lean` leaf that imports `GradingInteger`, so users importing the integer grading API do not get its marking-swap simp facts.

summary: The J-function and rational grading swap facts are now in their natural files, but the integer grading swap facts are still isolated in a late leaf file instead of the integer grading API they elaborate.
